fields.description:
## Description
LOC/size analyzer that shells `scc --format json` (deterministic, no network) and populates `AnalyzerResult.loc` (per-file/per-language LOC + max_loc). Absent scc -> `Unavailable`, never fabricated zeros.

## Approach & rationale (G6)
Extract the EXISTING scc JSON parse from `src/rebar/grounding/engine_b_backends.py` (`_metric_invoke`/`_scc_files`) into one shared helper `rebar.metrics.analyzers._scc.parse_scc_json`, then have BOTH callers use it. Rationale: a single normalizer is lower blast-radius and avoids a second, drifting scc-JSON parser — preferred over writing a fresh parser in scc_loc.py. No novel architecture (T1): this reuses the established in-repo scc shell-out pattern; scc is a mature OSS CLI already invoked by the grounding backend.

## Grounding (E4/G1G2 — verified via Grep/Read)
- CONFIRMED: `grep -n "\"--format\", \"json\"" src/rebar/grounding/engine_b_backends.py` shows the existing scc JSON shell-out at :376; `_scc_files(data)` at :412 normalizes per-file LOC.
- New files CREATED by this ticket: `src/rebar/metrics/analyzers/scc_loc.py`, `src/rebar/metrics/analyzers/_scc.py`. `AnalyzerResult`/`Unavailable(reason, accruing_since)` come from ticket 3b30 (depends_on). Consumer updated OUTSIDE this dir: `engine_b_backends.py` (re-pointed at the shared helper).

## Acceptance criteria (each an observable outcome with a proving command)
- [ ] `parse_scc_json` exists in `analyzers/_scc.py` and returns per-file LOC + max_loc. PROVE: `pytest -q tests/unit/metrics/test_scc_loc.py::test_parse_scc_json_shape`.
- [ ] `engine_b_backends` uses the SAME parser (single normalizer). PROVE: `pytest -q tests/unit/metrics/test_scc_loc.py::test_single_parser_reused` which asserts `import rebar.grounding.engine_b_backends as g, rebar.metrics.analyzers._scc as s; assert g._scc_files.__module__ == s.__name__` (grounding delegates to the shared symbol).
- [ ] scc on PATH: `scc_loc.analyze(root)` returns an `AnalyzerResult` whose `.loc.max_loc` equals the fixture max. PROVE (integration, skipif scc absent): `pytest -q tests/unit/metrics/test_scc_loc.py::test_live_scc -m integration`.
- [ ] scc ABSENT (monkeypatched runner raising FileNotFoundError): returns `Unavailable(reason, accruing_since=_ACCRUING_SINCE)`, NOT zeros, NOT a raise. PROVE: `pytest -q tests/unit/metrics/test_scc_loc.py::test_absent_scc_unavailable`.

## Dependencies: 3b30, 9597, e7a0 (recorded depends_on edges).
## Files: src/rebar/metrics/analyzers/scc_loc.py (new), src/rebar/metrics/analyzers/_scc.py (new), src/rebar/grounding/engine_b_backends.py (delegate to shared), tests/unit/metrics/test_scc_loc.py (new)
## Scope: M
